title: Lesson 4 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Hey! 👋 Lesson 4 — start of the second half of the course!
Four things today: **family**, **hobbies**, how to say **someone's something**, and a Maltese trick called **attached pronouns**. Three minutes. Let's go!
First — quick pattern. The masculine and feminine often **only differ by the last vowel**. **U for him. A for her.**
Heads up — **the plural for both 'sons' and 'daughters' is the same word**:
To say what you like, start with:
I like... and add the activity.
Spot the **n** at the start? That's the prefix for 'I'. We'll see way more about that in Lesson 5. 🪄
Quick one. To show **possession** in Maltese, use the little word:
So 'Sara's dog' becomes **'the dog of Sara'**:
Same word order as English '**the dog of Sara**' — but in Maltese it's the **default** way.
Now for the pronouns — my, your, his, her, our, their. Full forms first. 📌
**This is the powerful one.** Instead of saying 'the house of mine', Maltese **attaches the pronoun directly** to the noun as a suffix.
The suffixes: **-i, -ek, -u, -ha, -na, -kom, -hom**. Same set, every time.
Works on body parts too. My heart:
Once it clicks — Maltese feels **way smoother**. ⚡
Family. Hobbies. Possessive ta'. The attached-pronoun trick. That's lesson 4.
Tap a section, drill, repeat. You're really moving now. 💪
